statement.markdown: Given a string `s`, determine if it is a pangram, which is a sentence that uses all the letters of the alphabet at least once (case insensitive). Note that a pangram may contain non-alphabetic characters. If the string is a pangram, return `True`, otherwise return `False`.
statement.inputFormat: The input string `s` (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)
statement.notes: The input string may contain punctuation, spaces, and special characters.

examples.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
examples.explanation: This string is a pangram because it contains all the letters of the alphabet.
This string does not contain all the letters of the alphabet.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.betterSolution: Using a set to store unique alphabets found
editorial.optimalSolution: Using ASCII values of characters to find unique alphabets

testCases.input:
5
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
